data.title: Silver prices continued to fluctuate, with Gate XAG ranking among the top three globally in 24-hour trading volume.
data.description: Recently, international silver prices have continued their volatile pattern, with the trading range widening further. Market data shows that silver opened at $75.109 today, reaching a high of $77.286, and is currently trading at $76.760, a gain of 2.19%. The price center continues to rise, and market sentiment remains active. Influenced by changes in macroeconomic expectations and safe-haven demand, silver, with its dual industrial and metal attributes, has once again become a focus of capital attention. According to CoinGlass data, the 24-hour trading volume of XAG (silver) on the Gate platform reached $194 million, a gain of 91.72%, ranking among the top three globally, demonstrating Gate's depth and liquidity advantage in the metals sector. Overall, silver trading activity has significantly increased, and the liquidity structure of mainstream platforms remains robust. Gate TradFi has officially launched its trading platform, available via both an app and a web browser. It covers traditional financial trading sectors including stocks, metals, forex, indices, and commodities, supporting a wide range of trading instruments such as gold, silver, Tesla, Nvidia, and Apple. Users can enjoy leverage up to 500x and a highly competitive fee structure. Through continuous product innovation and infrastructure upgrades, Gate aims to accelerate the deep integration of crypto platforms with traditional financial markets, creating a professional-grade TradFi platform for global users.

data.title: XRP Price Walks a Tightrope As Downside Threat Persists
data.description:
XRP price failed to surpass $1. 4650 and started another decline. The price is now correcting gains and might struggle to stay above $1. 320
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/xrp-price-walks-a-tightrope-1-34/

data.title: Malaysia's Economic Growth Surpasses Expectations in Fourth Quarter
data.description: Malaysia's economy experienced a stronger-than-anticipated expansion in the fourth quarter, reinforcing its position as a robust growth driver in Southeast Asia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's economic resilience amid global uncertainties. The growth figures exceeded analysts' predictions, showcasing Malaysia's ability to navigate economic challenges effectively. This performance underscores the nation's strategic economic policies and its capacity to maintain momentum in a competitive regional landscape. The positive economic indicators reflect Malaysia's potential for sustained growth, contributing to its standing as a key player in Southeast Asia's economic development.

data.title: Minor International Considers Hong Kong IPO for Restaurant Business
data.description: Thailand's Minor International, a leading hospitality group in Asia, is reportedly exploring the possibility of launching an initial public offering (IPO) for its restaurant division in Hong Kong. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move comes as Bangkok's stock market seeks to rejuvenate domestic activity. The potential IPO reflects Minor International's strategic efforts to expand its financial footprint and capitalize on opportunities in the Hong Kong market. The company is known for its extensive portfolio in the hospitality sector, and this development could mark a significant step in its growth trajectory.

data.title: Bangchak to Acquire Chevron's Hong Kong Fuel Business for $270 Million
data.description: Bangchak Corporation has announced its acquisition of Chevron's fuel business in Hong Kong for $270 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of Bangchak's strategy to enhance its international presence. The Thai energy company aims to expand its operations beyond domestic borders, reflecting its commitment to growth in the global energy market. This acquisition marks a significant step in Bangchak's efforts to strengthen its position in the Asian energy sector.

data.title: Brookfield and GIC Pursue $1.96 Billion Loan for National Storage REIT Acquisition
data.description:
Brookfield Asset Management and Singapore's GIC are reportedly in the process of securing a $1.96 billion loan to support their acquisition of National Storage REIT, which is listed in Sydney. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the matter, that the loan is intended to facilitate the purchase of the Australian real estate investment trust.

The acquisition of National Storage REIT is part of a strategic move by Brookfield and GIC to expand their presence in the storage sector. National Storage REIT is one of the largest self-storage operators in Australia, offering significant growth potential in the region.

The loan, if secured, will provide the necessary financial backing for the transaction, allowing Brookfield and GIC to finalize the acquisition. The deal underscores the continued interest in the real estate sector, particularly in storage facilities, which have seen increased demand.

Brookfield Asset Management, a global alternative asset manager, and GIC, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund, have previously collaborated on various investment ventures. Their partnership in acquiring National Storage REIT highlights their commitment to investing in high-potential assets.

The acquisition is expected to enhance the operational capabilities and market reach of National Storage REIT, benefiting from the expertise and resources of both Brookfield and GIC. As the deal progresses, it will be closely watched by industry analysts and investors, given its potential impact on the real estate market in Australia.

data.title: Thailand's Conservative Party Seeks Coalition with Rival Pheu Thai
data.description: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's conservative party emerged victorious in Thailand's general election held on Sunday. Bloomberg posted on X that the party intends to invite the rival Pheu Thai party to form a coalition government. This move aims to consolidate power and ensure stability in the country's political landscape. The election results have positioned the conservative party as a dominant force, prompting discussions on potential alliances to govern effectively. The invitation to Pheu Thai reflects a strategic approach to unify differing political factions and address national challenges collaboratively. The outcome of these negotiations could significantly impact Thailand's future political direction.

data.title: Indonesian Corporates Anticipated to Continue Dollar Bond Sales Despite Moody's Downgrade
data.description: Citigroup forecasts that Indonesian companies will proceed with dollar bond issuances this year. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that this expectation persists despite Moody's Ratings recently downgrading its outlook on Indonesia's government credit score earlier this month. The decision by Moody's has raised concerns about the country's economic stability, yet Citigroup remains optimistic about the corporate sector's ability to navigate these challenges. The bank believes that Indonesian firms will continue to seek opportunities in the dollar bond market, leveraging favorable conditions and investor interest. This outlook reflects a broader trend among emerging markets, where companies are increasingly tapping into international debt markets to secure funding. Citigroup's analysis suggests that the demand for dollar bonds remains robust, driven by attractive yields and the need for capital to support growth initiatives. As Indonesian corporates move forward with their plans, they are expected to contribute significantly to the overall activity in the global bond market this year.

data.title: Octopus Group to Invest $14.2 Billion in Australian Renewable Projects
data.description: Octopus Group's Australian division has announced plans to invest up to $14.2 billion in renewable energy projects over the next five years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this as one of the most significant investment initiatives in Australia's green energy sector. The move underscores the growing commitment to sustainable energy solutions in the region, aiming to enhance the country's renewable energy infrastructure and reduce carbon emissions. This investment is expected to play a crucial role in advancing Australia's transition to a more sustainable energy future.

data.title: Tether Gold Introduces Scudo for Enhanced Gold Transactions
data.description:
On February 13, Tether Gold announced the launch of a new measurement unit, Scudo, to provide an innovative and intuitive way to measure the value of gold on the blockchain. According to BlockBeats, 1 Scudo is equivalent to 1/1000 of an XAUT (gold ounce), offering users a practical and manageable method for small-scale transactions, making the sending and receiving of gold on the blockchain more accessible.

Tether stated that this initiative aims to enhance the everyday usability of digital gold assets, providing investors and traders with a more flexible operational experience.

data.title: Australia's Reporting Season Intensifies with Rio Tinto and BHP Group in Focus
data.description: Australia's reporting season is set to accelerate next week, with major companies Rio Tinto and BHP Group expected to play a significant role in the nation's earnings recovery. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of these mining giants in the broader economic landscape. Analysts are closely monitoring the performance of these firms, as their results could provide insights into the health of the Australian economy. The mining sector, a key component of Australia's financial ecosystem, is anticipated to influence market sentiment and investor confidence. As the reporting season progresses, stakeholders are eager to assess the impact of global economic conditions on these industry leaders. The outcomes from Rio Tinto and BHP Group will be pivotal in shaping expectations for the country's financial trajectory.

data.title: Synera Renewable Energy Pursues $800 Million for Taiwan Wind Farm Project
data.description: Synera Renewable Energy, backed by Stonepeak Partners, is reportedly seeking $800 million in private credit to fund its offshore wind farm project in Taiwan. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's efforts to expand green power supply in the region. This move aligns with Taiwan's broader initiative to enhance its renewable energy infrastructure. The project is part of a growing trend in the country to invest in sustainable energy solutions, reflecting global shifts towards environmentally friendly power sources. Synera's pursuit of substantial funding underscores the increasing demand for clean energy and the financial strategies companies are employing to support such developments.

data.title: This week's mining data highlights: Bitcoin mining difficulty saw its largest single decrease since the summer of 2021; Cango raised $75.5 million to focus on its transformation into an AI distributed computing platform.
data.description: Week 7 of 2026 (February 6 - February 12): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 1030 EH/s, with a high of 1116 EH/s and a low of 901 EH/s, an increase of 9.28% compared to the previous week's average hashrate (912 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $68,401, with a high of $72,232 and a low of $60,001, a decrease of 13.18% compared to the previous week's average price ($78,781). 3. Notable mining news includes: (1) Bitcoin mining difficulty saw its largest single drop since the summer of 2021, with the seven-day average hashrate across the network falling to 990.08 EH/s; (2) Bitdeer disclosed that it mined 668 BTC in January, and its total Bitcoin holdings reached 1,530 at the end of January; (3) Canaan Technology mined 83 BTC in January, and its crypto reserves reached 1,778 BTC and 3,951 ETH; (4) Cango raised an additional $75.5 million to focus on its transformation into an AI distributed computing platform. Data partner: NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company Cango Inc. (CANG).

data.title: Bitcoin open interest hits lows not seen since 2024: Is TradFi abandoning BTC?
data.description:
Bitcoin open interest falls to $34 billion as investor demand dries up and traders’ concerns shift to worrying US macroeconomic data. Is TradFi exiting BTC?

data.title: Bank of Japan Board Member Comments on Neutral Interest Rate
data.description: Naoki Tamura, a member of the Bank of Japan's Policy Board, stated that he has consistently believed Japan's neutral interest rate is at least around 1%. According to Jin10, Tamura emphasized that different measurement methods can lead to varying estimates of this rate. His comments highlight ongoing discussions about Japan's monetary policy and the challenges in determining the appropriate interest rate level. The neutral interest rate is a key factor in shaping economic policy, as it influences inflation and economic growth.

data.title: Long-Term Bitcoin Holders Face Increased Pressure Amid Market Trends
data.description:
Crypto KOL Murphy posted on X that the Long-Term Holder Realized Profit/Loss Ratio (LTH-RPRL) for Bitcoin has fallen below 1 for the first time in this cycle, a situation last observed on May 9, 2022. Additionally, the Long-Term Holder Spent Output Profit Ratio (LTH-SOPR) is also below 1, indicating that long-term holders are not only experiencing losses but are also selling at a loss.

For those who have not yet sold, each fluctuation in Bitcoin's price presents a growing psychological challenge, as the sensitivity of long-term holders to price changes has reached levels similar to those on November 29, 2014, November 3, 2018, and May 25, 2022. Historically, these points have been followed by events that exacerbated panic among long-term holders, marking the final phase of a bear market.

Currently, the market sentiment is characterized by ignoring positive news and amplifying negative developments. Bitcoin appears to be increasingly desensitized to macroeconomic benefits, seemingly influenced by a mysterious force. This force is not controlled by any individual, institution, or entity but rather reflects a consensus issue among the majority of market participants.

Historically, when the LTH-RPRL drops below 1, the probability of Bitcoin entering a prolonged bear market increases significantly. This phase is not defined by the depth of price drops but by the duration of the downturn.

The final phase of a bear market is marked by extreme panic among long-term holders. The key indicator for this is the Entity-Adjusted Long-Term Holder Realized Loss (EA-LTH RL). The larger the scale, the greater the panic among long-term holders. On February 5, when Bitcoin fell to $60,000, the EA-LTH RL reached $680 million, surpassing the panic levels triggered by the LUNA crash in the previous bear market.

Whether this marks the final phase of the bear market remains uncertain. However, if Bitcoin prices drop further and the EA-LTH RL decreases, it could signal the end. If not, as long as the LTH-RPRL remains below 1, opportunities will persist.

data.title: Crossmint Unveils OpenClaw Payment Standard with Industry Support
data.description: Crossmint, a company specializing in crypto API development, has introduced the OpenClaw open payment standard through lobster.cash. According to Foresight News, this initiative is backed by Crossmint, Visa Smart Commerce, Solana, Circle, and Stytch. Users can set permissions and approve transactions, with the proxy requesting necessary resources only when needed. Cards and stablecoins are protected by programmable security measures by default. The service is currently available for trial and is expected to be fully launched in the coming weeks.

data.title: Trader Shifts Focus to U.S. Tech Stocks After Closing Major Positions
data.description:
On February 13, a prominent trader has shifted their investment focus to U.S. technology stocks after closing significant positions in the Nasdaq index and copper, valued at $40 million. According to BlockBeats, the trader's current holdings amount to approximately $23.5 million, primarily concentrated in sectors related to storage and artificial intelligence.

The trader's positions include a $9.31 million investment in Micron Technology (MU) with an average price of $379.993, yielding a profit of around $830,000. Additionally, they hold a $5.36 million position in NVIDIA (NVDA) at an average price of $192.858, currently incurring a loss of approximately $140,000. Their investment in Alphabet (GOOGL) stands at $4.63 million with an average price of $312.51, resulting in a loss of about $50,000. Lastly, the trader has a $3.19 million stake in SanDisk (SNDK) at an average price of $592.31, generating a profit of approximately $230,000.

Previously, the trader was known for their significant investments in U.S. stocks and on-chain precious metals, notably being the largest long position holder in on-chain gold. They had heavily invested in the Nasdaq index and copper prices with a $40 million stake before transitioning their focus to the technology sector.

data.title: Plume and EX.IO Forge Strategic Partnership to Advance RWA in Hong Kong
data.description: Plume, a network focused on real-world assets (RWA), has entered into a strategic partnership with licensed digital asset platform EX.IO to facilitate the large-scale implementation of institutional-level RWA in Hong Kong. According to Foresight News, this collaboration aims to leverage Plume's on-chain asset infrastructure and ecosystem network alongside EX.IO's compliant trading system and local market resources. The partnership seeks to establish a comprehensive channel for issuance, compliance, and trading, enabling high-quality assets to efficiently enter the on-chain capital market. This initiative is expected to further solidify Hong Kong's position as a global hub for RWA and digital financial innovation.
